---
title: "Sang B. Mendy"
description: "Mathematician and Data Scientist from The Gambia, 
  currently pursuing a Master's degree in Data Science and 
  Statistics in Indonesia. Sharing everything I learn about 
  R, Python, statistics and machine learning."
image: images/sang1.jpeg
about:
  template: jolla
  image-width: 20em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/YOUR-TWITTER-HANDLE
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/YOUR-LINKEDIN
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/sbaptist
    - icon: envelope
      text: Contact me
      href: mailto:sangbm7@gmail.com
---

Hello and welcome — I am glad you are here.

My name is **Sang B. Mendy**, a mathematician turned data scientist from **The Gambia**, currently pursuing a **Master's degree in Data Science and Statistics** in Indonesia. My journey has taken me from the classrooms of the University of The Gambia all the way to Southeast Asia — and along the way, I have discovered that data, when truly understood, has the power to transform lives and communities.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What I Do

I sit at the intersection of **mathematics, statistics, and data science**. My work involves turning raw numbers into meaningful insights — through statistical modelling, machine learning, and data visualization.

My core tools are:

-   **R** — for statistical analysis, visualization, and reporting
-   **Python** — for machine learning and data manipulation
-   **Quarto & R Markdown** — for reproducible research and documentation
-   **MS Excel** — for quick data exploration and reporting
-   **PowerBI** 
-   **Minitab**
-   **SQL**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What This Website Is About

This website is my **open notebook** — a living record of everything I learn as a student, a researcher, and a data enthusiast. You will find:

-   **Blog posts** — honest reflections on statistics, data science, and life as an African student in Asia
-   **Tutorials** — step-by-step guides on R, Python, and statistical methods
-   **Projects** — real analyses and research I have worked on
-   **Resources** — the best books, courses, and tools I recommend

I believe that the best way to truly understand something is to teach it. So I write — not because I know everything, but because I am learning, and I want to bring you along for the journey.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## My Story

I grew up in The Gambia with a love for mathematics — the precision of it, the elegance of it, the way a single equation could describe something vast and complex. That love led me to pursue a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, which then opened the door to the world of data science.

Today, I am studying in Indonesia — thousands of miles from home, immersed in a new culture, a new language, and an exciting new chapter of learning. Living abroad has taught me things that no textbook could: resilience, adaptability, and the beauty of seeing the world through different eyes.

I believe that **where you come from does not limit where you can go** — and I hope this website is proof of that.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Currently

-   Master's student in Data Science & Statistics in Indonesia
-   Learning advanced statistical modelling and machine learning
-   Building this website as a public learning journal
-   Open to collaborations, research opportunities, and conversations about data

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*"The goal is not to be perfect. The goal is to keep learning."*
